item.title: Re: Staff Post - Open
item.content:encoded: I dig it.

item.title: Staff Post - Open
item.content:encoded:
[timer redacted]

The WikiWalk team is proposing edits to this page. All proposed edits will be in a collapsible below this post, and after 7 days if there are no serious objections to the edits, the feedback received will be taken into account as the edits are made. If you have questions not directly related to the article about this project, please pm a member of the WikiWalk Team

+ show block
– hide block

I propose the following edit:

A variety of abandoned buildings in varying states of disrepair. SCP-1448 has attempted to inhabit SCP-████ on five separate occasions, in each case compounding [REDACTED].

To this:

A variety of abandoned buildings in varying states of disrepair. SCP-1448 has attempted to inhabit SCP-1084 on five separate occasions, in each case compounding [REDACTED].

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: That is… not an interpretation I had considered before. You might be onto something with that.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: The idea of a con-artist haunted place is really interesting.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: At first I wondered it it was the anomaly, but it just appears as a black blob, so that wasn't right.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
It was in the photo when I found it — it's the photographer, if I remember correctly. The rest of the photo was so perfect for it that I decided to just leave it there as an 'Easter egg', to maybe raise some questions.

Maybe it's a statue or something, I dunno.

I think that this post is the first time in nearly four years that anyone's mentioned it.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I just discovered this one, definitely underrated I think. I am a little confused by the image though. What is the human-shaped figure on the left hand side? A statue? A person standing on a stump, related to the "ritual"?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I upvoted this HARD.

Also, this reminds me of Goosebumps for some reason.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I'm glad you like it! :3 I hope I captured the feel of the article for you and everyone else who's a fan of this!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Decibelle made me this for the gift exchange. It's about this article, and somehow also cool!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Stealthy butterflies.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Because it's not necessary (from an writing standpoint) to the story told by this SCP. Mentioning it, however, is a form of "World Building," indicating that there's more information and helping to give a "genuine" or "realistic" feel for this article.

-EDIT- Bwa! Zynja'd! I swear, if I ever discover a species of stealthy butterflies, I'm naming it after you, Zyn!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Because the author didn't feel like writing it? A few articles have mentions of yet-to-be-written-IRL supplements; it's sort of the in-universe immersion aspect. SCP articles are comparable to scientific papers, which can have tons of citations.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: See Incident Report 1448-38 <-— so why does this not exist?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: what is nuntii?

item.title: Picture Attribution
item.content:encoded:
EDIT: The image has been replaced.

The new image is by Larkwind on Flickr, original here, under a CC BY 2.0 license.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Yoric, it's observations like these that made you endlessly useful, your abrasive moments aside. It is really too bad that you've gone.

item.title: Re: Some updates
item.content:encoded: ffffff. Fixing.

item.title: Re: Some updates
item.content:encoded:
There's a little problem:

"…Every documented "ritual" involves situations in which . No blood lost by a subject at SCP-1448-1 has been recovered…"
Is there supposed to be a "data expunged" in there?

item.title: Some updates
item.content:encoded: I changed around the containment procedures a bit, streamlined a few things here and there, clarified some, and removed a paragraph where it became lolkillsyou. I also made a small attempt at giving it some sort of motive (if that can even work). If any of that would make you consider re-reading or changing your vote, please do so and leave a comment so I can know if I'm doing any good.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: That was intentional. When I first wrote Bees, I was trying to garner attention for this, seeing as how it was my lowest-rated SCP.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: BEE in hexadecimal is 1448 in decimal form. My favorite number. Who'da thunkit.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Man, I still like the writing enough to keep my upvote on there, but besides that I couldn't agree with yoric more here.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
So, if a place is creepy, and has probably had people die there, a cloud descends and people make up urban legends…?

As opposed to, yanno, people making up urban legends because they're creepy places where people have probably died?

I'm sorry, I can't help but read this as an overenthusiastic researcher missing the point and making up an SCP where there is none. The writing is fantastic, but the concept plus the overspecific list of qualifications negates that by giving me the acute impression someone has screwed up cause and effect. Downvoted.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
1. It wasn't based on any particular urban legend. I noticed that other people were writing 1000 contest entries that were, so it occurred to me that an SCP could be responsible for urban legends.

2. Being able to kill people repeatedly might not be Keter-worthy, but factor in that it's extremely difficult to track due to A. You can't lock it in a box so you have to follow it around B. being virtually invisible, C. moving very quickly and erratically, and D. if you lose track of it, you probably have to wait until it makes the news (which is bad) to find out where it went. Plus, if you don't get some vehicles chasing it as soon as it leaves, it's pretty much gone until you can figure out where it went.

In short, it's not the lethality that makes it Keter, it's that plus the extreme difficulty in containing it. It's like a serial killer who you can't put in jail or execute; you have to follow him around and convince people not to go into his house, which has tons of money on the front porch. Also, he's invisible.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
1. What urban legend was this based on? It seems to be an urban legend generator rather than an urban legend itself.
  2. What does it do that warrants a keter rating? As far as I can tell, the worst it can do is turn some of the kids in the area into serial killers, which is bad, but doesn't strike me as keter-worthy.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I made a few edits, mostly for wording. Picked a new task force to assist the Triple Dog Daredevils, removed some blackboxes, and simplified some parts that were just unnecessarily wordy.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
The rituals made by 1000 are all bullshit. Granted, it's oddly believable bullshit that goads you into doing dangerous things, but it just doesn't work. This is assuming there isn't anything more interesting behind the [DATA EXPUNGED].

That being said, any ideas as to what I can do to improve this (short of using a different core concept entirely)? It seems like most of the upvotes I have are just barely there, and I want this to be as good as I can make it.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Well it definately looks like you managed to SCP-ify the urban legend (or is it folktale?), so no bad stuff there.
And I can't recall any SCP that already does this stuff.
So it's a +1 from me.

Although it would have been nice to know what the behaviour of SCP-1000-1 was while in the presence of humans, like if they react to the rituals at all.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I am incredibly lukeward towards this. I think you did very well with it, better than I would have expected, but the core concept just doesn't do much to grab me. Barely upvoted (I give it a 6/10, 5 being a neutral vote).

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Thanks. I suppose we will agree to disagree, then.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
This is exceptionally well-written, especially taking into account that you wrote it under Bright's challenge conditions.

I don't really like the central concept. I don't like explaining interestingly creepy human behavior with an SCP that will never quite measure up.

I may just upvote this later, simply because I like how it's written (and I'm not sure how to fix the concept at the moment, and I'm not sure you should considering everyone else might love it as much as Rhett does), but for now, neutral vote.

EDIT: Oh fuck it, +1'ed

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Thank you!

(For reference, I'm still here for another hour or so).

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I love it

This, sir, is spectacular. My congratulations

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Oh hey look what I just shat out. I will be gone for a week or so, so I will not be able to answer your questions or address your concerns until that time. Feel free to edit for spelling and grammar and whatnot.

This is my attempt at writing an SCP in one day flat with no consultation. I had the idea before, but yeah. I just hope it doesn't get baleeted before I get back.

Otherwise, feel free to discuss and comment.
